title: Module 4: Performance Exception Report
summary: Build a grouped exception report with row filtering, HAVING thresholds, conditional KPIs, and performance labels.

sketchBlocks.title: Introduction to Performance Exception Reports
sketchBlocks.bodyMarkdown: In this project, you will create a Performance Exception Report using SQL. This report will help identify groups that require management attention by applying row filters, group filters, and conditional aggregation. You'll use the `sales_reporting` table from the `sales_kpi` dataset to build this report. The key concepts include using `WHERE` to filter rows before grouping, `HAVING` to filter groups after aggregation, and `CASE` statements for conditional aggregation.

quizDraft.title: Filter Rows with WHERE
quizDraft.prompt: Write a query to select all rows from the `sales_reporting` table where the `order_status` is 'Completed'.
quizDraft.hint: Use the WHERE clause to filter rows based on the order_status column.

help.concept: The WHERE clause filters rows before any grouping or aggregation.
help.hint_1: Check the order_status column for the value 'Completed'.
help.hint_2: Use WHERE order_status = 'Completed' to filter the rows.

tests.stdout:
order_id|order_date|region|category|product_name|sales_rep|quantity|unit_price|discount_pct|order_status|customer_rating

quizDraft.title: Group and Filter with HAVING
quizDraft.prompt: Write a query to find the total quantity sold for each `region` where the total quantity is greater than 20.
quizDraft.hint: Use GROUP BY to aggregate by region and HAVING to filter the groups.

help.concept: The HAVING clause filters groups after aggregation.
help.hint_1: Group by the region column and use SUM to calculate total quantity.
help.hint_2: Use HAVING SUM(quantity) > 20 to filter the groups.

quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT region, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region HAVING total_quantity > 20;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT region, SUM(quantity) AS total_quantity FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region HAVING total_quantity > 20;

quizDraft.title: Conditional Aggregation with CASE
quizDraft.prompt: Write a query to count the number of 'Completed' and 'Pending' orders for each `category`.
quizDraft.hint: Use CASE inside COUNT to conditionally count orders based on their status.

help.concept: CASE can be used inside aggregate functions for conditional aggregation.
help.hint_1: Use COUNT with CASE to count 'Completed' and 'Pending' separately.
help.hint_2: Use COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) for completed orders.

quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT category, COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders, COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Pending' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY category;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT category, COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) AS completed_orders, COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Pending' THEN 1 END) AS pending_orders FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY category;

quizDraft.title: Label Performance with CASE
quizDraft.prompt: Write a query to label each `region` as 'High Performance' if the average `customer_rating` is above 4.5, otherwise 'Needs Improvement'.
quizDraft.hint: Use CASE to assign labels based on the average customer rating.

help.concept: CASE can be used to assign labels based on conditions.
help.hint_1: Calculate the average customer_rating for each region.
help.hint_2: Use CASE WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance' ELSE 'Needs Improvement' END.

quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT region, CASE WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance' ELSE 'Needs Improvement' END AS performance_label FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT region, CASE WHEN AVG(customer_rating) > 4.5 THEN 'High Performance' ELSE 'Needs Improvement' END AS performance_label FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region;

tests.stdout:
region|performance_label
North|Needs Improvement
South|High Performance
East|Needs Improvement
West|Needs Improvement

quizDraft.title: Combine Filters and Labels
quizDraft.prompt: Write a query to find regions with more than 3 'Completed' orders and label them as 'Active'.
quizDraft.hint: Combine HAVING with CASE to filter and label groups.

help.concept: Combine HAVING and CASE to filter and label groups based on conditions.
help.hint_1: Use HAVING to filter regions with more than 3 completed orders.
help.hint_2: Use CASE to label these regions as 'Active'.

quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT region, CASE WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) > 3 THEN 'Active' ELSE 'Inactive' END AS activity_status FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region HAVING activity_status = 'Active';
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT region, CASE WHEN COUNT(CASE WHEN order_status = 'Completed' THEN 1 END) > 3 THEN 'Active' ELSE 'Inactive' END AS activity_status FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region HAVING activity_status = 'Active';

quizDraft.title: Final Performance Exception Report
quizDraft.prompt: Create a final report that lists each `region` with the total sales and labels them as 'Exceptional' if total sales exceed $500, otherwise 'Standard'.
quizDraft.hint: Use SUM to calculate total sales and CASE to label regions based on sales.

help.concept: Use aggregate functions and CASE to create a comprehensive report.
help.hint_1: Calculate total sales for each region using SUM(quantity * unit_price).
help.hint_2: Use CASE to label regions based on whether total sales exceed $500.

quizDraft.starterCode: SELECT region, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales, CASE WHEN total_sales > 500 THEN 'Exceptional' ELSE 'Standard' END AS performance_label FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region;
quizDraft.solutionCode: SELECT region, SUM(quantity * unit_price) AS total_sales, CASE WHEN total_sales > 500 THEN 'Exceptional' ELSE 'Standard' END AS performance_label FROM sales_reporting GROUP BY region;
